description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, angular profile and a predominantly grayish-brown coloration. The surface is coarse and uneven, featuring natural fractures, jagged edges, and some flat planes, with visible mineral striations and subtle color variations. Several faces of the stone display hand-written alphanumeric markings in black ink, suggesting it may have been cataloged or sampled for study. The stone's mass and density are apparent from its solid, blocky form, and it appears to be composed entirely of natural rock material. Its weathered exterior shows signs of exposure to the elements, with minor chips and rough patches but no significant moss or lichen growth. The object would be a typical sight as a barrier or obstacle in urban parks, roadside verges, or landscaped public spaces.
description: A rugged, grayish-brown stone with jagged edges, visible mineral striations, and hand-written markings on its surface. Its coarse, weathered texture and irregular shape make it a natural obstacle or barrier.
description_view_0: This view shows the stone's broadest, flattest face with a sloped top and a visible hand-written marking on the right side.
description_view_1: From this angle, the stone's angular, pointed end is prominent, with two visible faces displaying catalog numbers.
description_view_2: This perspective highlights the largest flat surface with the marking 'LB42' clearly visible, and shows the stone's layered texture.
description_view_3: The stone appears more compact and blocky from this view, with rough, fractured surfaces and a smaller marking visible near the top.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Natural mineral striations and subtle layering, with hand-written catalog markings; no regular artificial patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with minor chips and surface abrasions, indicating moderate exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural ground cover or gravel, but the hand-written markings may contrast with more natural surroundings.
